LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JAMES KILPATRICK, JORDAN GELLER AND KIMO, INC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS ON THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF WRITTEN CONTRACT IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF INSUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF A WRITTEN CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO ATTACH THE LISTING AGREEMENT TO HIS SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND TO ALLEGE, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS SHOWING EITHER THAT AN EXPRESS TERM OF THAT CONTRACT WAS BREACHED AND/OR AN IMPLIED TERM OF THAT CONTRACT WAS BREACHED. IN DOING SO, PLAINTIFF MUST IDENTIFY WITH SPECIFICITY THE TERM OR TERMS HE ALLEGES WERE BREACHED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JAMES KILPATRICK, JORDAN GELLER AND KIMO, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND ATTORNEY'S FEES ALLEGATIONS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANTS WITHDREW THEIR MOTION AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS AND, IN ALL EVENTS, AS DETERMINED BY A PRIOR ORDER, THOSE ALLEGATIONS ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. PLAINTIFF PHILIP SHERIDAN HAS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED ENTITLEMENT TO FEES PER THE TORT OF ANOTHER DOCTRINE. NOTHING IN ANY OF THE PRIOR ORDERS PRECLUDES MR. SHERIDAN FROM ALLEGING FEES UNDER THE TORT OF ANOTHER DOCTRINE. BOTH SIDES' REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED SINCE NEITHER SIDE COMPLIED WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 128.7. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/HK

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SAM WALKER, TEL. NO. 1-213-364-0224 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ANDREW FOSTER SHI OF THE MANNING & KASS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-217-6990 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAMES KILPATRICK, JORDAN GELLER and KIMO, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendants' motion to strike the punitive damages and attorney's fees allegations in the second amended complaint is denied in its entirety. Defendants withdrew their motion as to the punitive damages allegations and, in all events, as determined by a prior order, those allegations are adequately alleged. Plaintiff Philip Sheridan has adequately alleged entitlement to fees per the tort of another doctrine. Nothing in any of the prior orders precludes Mr. Sheridan from alleging fees under the tort of another doctrine. Both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are denied since neither side complied with the requirements of CCP 128.7. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Apr-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAMES KILPATRICK, JORDAN GELLER and KIMO, INC's Motion To Strike the attorney's fees allegations in the first amended complaint is granted with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff insufficiently alleges entitlement to contractual attorney's fees. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to attach the listing agreement to his second amended complaint and to allege, if he can do so in good faith, facts showing that, per the attorney's fees clause in that agreement, that he is entitled to recover his attorney's fees. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAMES KILPATRICK, JORDAN GELLER and KIMO, INC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings on the fourth cause of action for breach of written contract in the first amended complaint is granted with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiff insufficiently alleges a claim for breach of a written contract. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to attach the listing agreement to his second amended complaint and to allege, if he can do so in good faith, facts showing either that an express term of that contract was breached and/or an implied term of that contract was breached. In doing so, plaintiff must identify with specificity the term or terms he alleges were breached. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Continued To Apr-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants James Kilpatrick, Jordan Geller, And Kimo, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants James Kilpatrick, Jordan Geller and Kimo, Inc.'s Demurrer To Plaintiff's Complaint -
(1) Defendants' Demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to Plaintiff's breach of contract claim, cause of action 4. Plaintiff fails to state with specificity how Defendants breached the Listing Agreement. Plaintiff also fails to state what Defendant's obligations were under the Listing Agreement.
(2) Defendants' Demurrer is overruled as to Plaintiff's fraud claim, cause of action 2. "The failure of the fiduciary to disclose a material fact to his principal which might affect the fiduciary's motives or the principal's decision, which is known (or should be known) to the fiduciary, may constitute constructive fraud. According to Plaintiff, Defendants pressured him into selling his two Marin rental properties. They did not tell him how the low sale prices were set. Defendants did not tell him that he would have to finance the sale of his properties. Defendants did not tell him that the buyer had no bank support and tried to keep him from meeting the buyer, who was allegedly intoxicated. Based on these allegations, an intent to defraud can be inferred. Defendants allegedly failed to disclose facts that would have been material to Plaintiff's decision to proceed with the sale and financing of his sale.
(3) Defendants' Demurrer is overruled as to Plaintiff's unfair business practices claim, cause of action 6. " 'Section 17200 "is not confined to anticompetitive business practices, but is also directed toward the public's right to protection from fraud, deceit, and unlawful conduct. [Citation.]" ' " Accepting Plaintiff's allegations as true, the public is likely to be deceived from Defendant's alleged acts and omissions. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Defendants James Kilpatrick, Jordan Geller, And Kimo, Inc.'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants James Kilpatrick, Jordan Geller and Kimo, Inc.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint -
(1) Defendants' Motion to Strike is granted without prejudice to amend the complaint as to Plaintiff's claim for attorney's fees. Attorney's fees are unavailable without a contract or statute. Plaintiff does not allege that the Listing Agreement included a provision for attorney's fees, nor does he attach the Listing Agreement to his Complaint. Even though attorney's fees are available in a dispute between Plaintiff and Pebbles, that does not mean that attorney's fees are necessarily available in the instant action between Plaintiff and Defendants.
(2) Defendants' Motion to Strike is granted without prejudice to amend the complaint as to Plaintiff's claim for restitution. Plaintiff seeks restitution but does not allege that Defendants earned a commission or other material benefit. Restitution, therefore, is improper.
(3) Defendants' Motion to Strike is granted without prejudice to amend the complaint as to Plaintiff's claim for punitive damages based on breach of contract and implied indemnity, cause of action 4 and 5. Punitive damages are not available in a contract action. (Civ. Code, 3294.)
(4) Defendants' Motion to Strike is denied as to Plaintiff's claim for punitive damages for breach of fiduciary duty, fraud, negligence and unfair competition, cause of action 1, 2, 3 and 6. Defendants allegedly failed to disclose facts that would have been material to Plaintiff's decision to proceed with the sale and financing of his sale. According to Plaintiff, Defendants pressured him into selling his two Marin rental properties. They did not tell him how the low sale prices were set. Defendants did not tell him that he would have to finance the sale of his properties. Defendants did not tell him that the buyer had no bank support and tried to keep him from meeting the buyer, who was allegedly intoxicated. Malice can be inferred from these allegations. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
